replies.author: @TonnyKrain
replies.text:
Cant wait.
Its like you know how you have an insane fanfiction story in your head, and you want to show it to the world, but making the animation of your fanfic idea will take years and you kinda give up.

Now you have the tools to transfer your ideas into animations in real time and show the world your version of this or that movie, without investing years into it. 
AI is an amazing tool for productivity and time saving.

author: @PatchworkTweed
text:
I finally watched Ep 9 for the second time (first time for my kids) and it was clear they were trying to backpedal to mitigate Ep 8 and catch up to their intended conclusion.

I think they need to just clear the air and declare 7-8-9 to be legends. The planets exist, the characters exist, but that's not quite how it all went down.

Then draw from the good ideas in the OG legends and build good, coherent stories before and after the sequel timeline that stay true to the original characters as well.

text: Honestly, they could bring Ben and Luke back the same way. If I remember correctly it in one of the versions of ROJ that when Luke and Obi-wan were having their talk. Granted that means that anyone that learned how to become one with the Force can return. But that would be a problem for another day. In truth what you'd need to figure out is why was this person sent back or chose to come back. Did they see something coming that they were trying to stop?
